seoDescription: Paraguay's lowest-cost route: residency via a film/audiovisual contribution from USD 40,000, through a separate INAP pathway. How it works and the honest caveats. Contact Lincoln.

faqs.question: How much does the film route cost?
faqs.answer: It requires a USD 40,000 contribution into an approved Paraguayan film production, plus separately demonstrating at least USD 80,000 in available bank funds (a show-of-funds, not a second investment). It is the lowest-cost entry point to Paraguayan residency.

faqs.question: Do I get the money back?
faqs.answer: You should treat the USD 40,000 as a contribution, not a recoverable investment. The funds remain in the production; any return depends entirely on the film’s commercial performance and is speculative. If you want a recoverable asset, the real estate, financial instruments, or SUACE routes fit better — and we will tell you so.

faqs.question: Is this part of the Investor Pass resolution?
faqs.answer: No. The film route is a separate, pre-existing pathway administered via Paraguay’s audiovisual institute (INAP), whose enabling statute is Ley N° 6106/2018. It sits alongside — not within — Resolution 0283/2026. The exact regulation granting the immigration benefit should be confirmed with counsel before you commit.

faqs.question: How fast is it, and how long must I be in Paraguay?
faqs.answer: Advisors indicate roughly 60 days from contribution and submission, with approximately eight business days in Asunción. We confirm the current requirements for your nationality before you travel.

faqs.question: Does my family get permanent residency too?
faqs.answer: An important distinction: the main applicant receives direct permanent residency, but dependent family members typically receive two-year temporary residency first, with the standard conversion pathway thereafter. We map this out for your family specifically.

blocks.eyebrow: Film cultural contribution · from USD 40,000 · separate INAP pathway
blocks.lead: Contribute USD 40,000 to qualifying Paraguayan film production through a separate, pre-existing pathway — the most accessible threshold to direct permanent residency, with the honest trade-offs stated plainly.

blocks.heading: The most accessible way in — clearly explained.

paragraphs.text: The film route qualifies you for direct permanent residency through a USD 40,000 contribution to an approved Paraguayan film production, channelled through a registered local production company. You also demonstrate at least USD 80,000 in available bank funds — a solvency check, not a second investment. At this threshold it is the lowest-cost entry point to Paraguayan residency.

Be precise about what this is. Unlike the resolution's investment routes, this is a contribution, not a recoverable asset. The funds remain in the production; any financial return is speculative. You are converting the smallest possible amount of capital into a clean path to residency — and you should view the USD 40,000 as the price of that path.

asideNotice.title: A separate pathway — verify the detail
asideNotice.body: This route sits alongside, not inside, Resolution 0283/2026. The precise regulation granting the immigration benefit is not publicly published; we confirm it with Paraguayan counsel before you commit.

blocks.heading: Lowest cost. Same destination — with caveats.

cards.title: Lowest entry point
cards.text: At USD 40,000 it is the most accessible threshold — the cheapest way to begin a Paraguayan residency file.

cards.title: Capital-efficient
cards.text: If your objective is the residency and the pathway to citizenship, this commits the least capital to get there.

cards.title: Direct PR for the applicant
cards.text: The main applicant receives direct permanent residency, bypassing the temporary stage.

cards.title: Simple to structure
cards.text: A contribution is administratively straightforward — no property to manage, no company to operate.

cards.title: Supports local industry
cards.text: Your contribution goes toward Paraguay's growing audiovisual sector rather than sitting idle.

cards.title: Eyes open
cards.text: Funds are not recoverable, dependents start on temporary residency, and ~8 days in-country are needed. We say so up front.

paragraphs.text: The film route is the most direct sequence in cost terms: make the qualifying contribution, assemble the file, spend the required days in-country, then receive residency — the same destination every route reaches.

notice.title: Timelines are indicative
notice.body: The ~60-day and ~8-business-day figures come from practitioners, not a published regulation. We confirm current requirements before you travel.

items.title: Strategy & fit
items.text: We confirm the film route is the right call — and are candid when an asset-backed route would serve you better.

items.title: Qualifying contribution
items.text: The USD 40,000 contribution to an approved production is arranged and documented; available funds are evidenced.

items.title: File assembly
items.text: We compile the complete application, including any Ministry of Foreign Affairs letter for visa-restricted nationalities.

items.title: Submission & travel
items.text: The file is submitted; roughly eight business days in Asunción are typically required.

items.title: Permanent residency
items.text: The main applicant's permanent residency is progressed — generally around 60 days from contribution and submission.

items.title: Toward citizenship
items.text: You maintain residence, building toward naturalisation eligibility after three years.

blocks.pull: The smallest step. The same destination.
